content.text:
{
  "dimension": "coaching-stance",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The coach operates from genuine VIEW throughout. Wonder is present and specific to the phenomenon in front of them — not generic 'what's underneath' probing, but precise attention to the laugh's exhaustion (T2), the metaphor (T8), the mid-sentence stop (T10), the 'huh' (T12), the thing that crossed her face (T14). Impartiality is strong: the coach refuses every invitation to collude — does not validate the disdain for the ex ('he just drifted... great, fantastic'), does not engage the partner-picking frame, does not bite on the attachment-theory diagnosis the client offers as a deflection. There is no agenda visible — the coach is not steering toward 'you choose this'; she lets the client arrive there herself in T15. Empathy is felt not performed — no filler validation noises, just precise reflection ('You can name the whole thing and still walk straight into it. That's its own kind of exhausting.'). Pace is the strongest signal: the coach repeatedly slows when the client speeds up ('Stay right there', 'Stay with that huh', 'Let that land'), and holds silence with the bare two-word intervention at T12. Vulnerability is the one component slightly less visible — the coach doesn't name her own state explicitly — but the undefendedness is evident in the willingness to drop questions entirely and just hold space. The coach does not need the client to land anywhere; the landing happens because nothing is being pushed.",
  "evidence_turns": [2, 6, 8, 10, 12, 14],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Opens with a precise noticing of the laugh rather than engaging the content of the complaint. Wonder for the actual phenomenon."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Reflects the fury without inflating it or colluding with the ex-disdain. Then redirects to feeling underneath the 'what's wrong with me' question — impartial but warm."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "The 'what happens in your body if you had what you say you want' question is exactly the breakthrough question from genuine curiosity, offered tentatively ('it might land a little sideways') — undefended, willing to be off."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Slows the client's acceleration. Catches the laugh-at-own-metaphor as defense and gently returns to 'relief, I could stop working so hard.' No agenda in the question — open."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Refuses the intellectual pivot to dad-theory. Stays with the actual live moment of the stopped sentence. Wonder for the present phenomenon, not for the story."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Three words. Holds silence. This is the clearest VIEW-stance moment — total trust that nothing more needs to be done."},
    {"turn": 14, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Again, minimal, present, follows the body. No interpretation offered, no rush to meaning."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["feeling-the-unfelt-emotion", "if-i-were-inherently-good"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["deconstruct-the-victim-to-release-anger", "i-dont-buy-your-story", "what-are-you-defending"],
  "notes": "Stance is excellent even where methodology fidelity is partial — the coach found her own path to the abyss-moment without needing the signature moves. Vulnerability/self-disclosure is the one VIEW component not explicitly visible, but its absence does not signal a controlled stance here; rather, the coach is simply not in the way."
}
